meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to implement data-driven analytics to identify market gaps and overcome the 'no project ideas' trap as a founder.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from a lack of market feedback loops. By implementing lightweight, event-based analytics, you transform passive observation into actionable product data. Use Antigravity to instrument your MVP and Claude Code to automate insights, turning raw user behavior into high-converting SaaS feature roadmaps.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks agonizing over the 'perfect' idea, yet the only thing you’ve shipped is a growing sense of impostor syndrome. The truth is, ideas don't come from inspiration; they come from observing friction in the wild. If you don't know what to build, it’s because you aren't listening to the data.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'guessing' to 'measuring.' By embedding an analytics layer into your development workflow, you stop building for yourself and start building for the market. This guide provides the technical infrastructure to turn anonymous traffic into a goldmine of project requirements.

steps.title: Instrument the User Journey
steps.description: Identify critical friction points by tracking events where users drop off. We focus on conversion funnels rather than vanity metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lightweight analytics wrapper for a Next.js application that tracks click events, page views, and form submission duration with minimal latency.

steps.title: Automate Insight Extraction
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse your raw event logs and identify the top three user complaints or abandoned workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Python script that aggregates JSON logs from my analytics provider and outputs a markdown report highlighting the highest churn segments.

steps.title: Map Features to Pain Points
steps.description: Cross-reference your data with existing market gaps to validate if your proposed solution has actual commercial viability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Architect. Analyze this user churn report and suggest three micro-SaaS features that solve the top identified friction point.

steps.title: Build the Lean Prototype
steps.description: Deploy a functional MVP component that specifically targets the validated pain point found in step 3.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a React component for a subscription-based feedback widget that integrates directly with my existing analytics event stream.

steps.title: Close the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Automate the collection of user sentiment post-interaction to ensure your new feature is actually solving the problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated workflow that triggers a sentiment analysis scan on user feedback logs and updates a Notion project board.

pro_tips: Never track everything; focus on 'North Star' events that correlate directly with user retention.
Use session replays selectively—they are great for debugging but privacy-heavy.
If your analytics show zero activity, stop building and start doing customer interviews immediately.
Always A/B test your CTA copy against the primary pain point discovered in your data.
When in doubt, prioritize features that reduce the time-to-value for the user.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior without making changes. Use the data to draft your first PRD, then leverage Claude Code to scaffold the MVP in under a day.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start building products that users are actually waiting for. Your next breakthrough is hiding in your data. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
